examples: These verbs mean to affect detrimentally.
A particularly difficult set of well conditions that may detrimentally affect steel, elastomers, mud additives, electronics, or tools and tool components.
Just because you might not smoke and hit the gym regularly, that doesn't mean there's not another sneaky habit that is detrimentally affecting your health without you even knowing.
"We know from questions asked in the Northern Ireland Life and Times Survey that displays of flags detrimentally affect people's use of facilities and shops in certain areas, " he said.
